bf16_result.error: RuntimeError: hipBLASLt returned no GroupedGemm solution
bf16_result.read: This ROCm 7.2.2 hipBLASLt GroupedGemm extension path does not expose a usable BF16 solution for the tested row-major A @ B.T owner-rank shape, even at a small control shape.

decision.next_moe_backend_lever: Do not wire hipBLASLt GroupedGemm into TorchTitan. Continue with CK Tile/TileLang/custom grouped-training-GEMM direction, preserving the current padded torch/Triton-SwiGLU owner-rank VJP as the baseline until a backend beats 6.43 ms or the 4.77 ms saved-output upper bound.
decision.scope_correction: The MoE backend can target the MoE slice of the 36.17 ms bucket, not attention sparse-MLA/LI Cijk or dense attention projection Cijk.
decision.why: No BF16 grouped solution in the active ROCm image for the tested shape, and the FP16 grouped solution is much slower than torch.bmm.

fp16_control.read: FP16 proves the binding and library call path work, but the selected grouped kernel is about 9.5x slower than torch.bmm for this contraction, so it is not a promising fallback even aside from BF16.

full_backward_cijk_context.read: The 36.17 ms full-backward Cijk bucket is mostly MoE routed-expert VJP in the old single-GPU packed-sim trace, but the EP8 owner-rank VJP probe is a different distributed shape and should not be compared to the whole bucket.

probe_scope: Benchmark-only raw grouped A @ B.T GEMM floor after operands are packed; excludes routing, SwiGLU, combine, top-k/router gradients, and token reduce.
question: Can ROCm hipBLASLt GroupedGemm be used/ported as the next backend for the MoE backward GEMM/BMM-family wall?
